title: silene uniflora subsp. prostrata
intro: Silene uniflora subsp. prostrata is a subspecies of the Silene uniflora plant, also known as the one-flowered catchfly, a flowering plant in the pink family (Caryophyllaceae). The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, particularly in the treatment of wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Silene uniflora subsp. prostrata include its ability to reduce inflammation, promote wound healing, and exhibit antimicrobial activity. Herbal preparations of the plant include infusions, tinctures, and salves, which can be used topically or internally to reap its medicinal benefits.
